decisions.rationale: Block p005_c0008 merely lists BRT toolbox features introduced to workshop participants. No explicit statement attaches these features to any specific corridor or project. The block simply lists features and amenities.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Facilitators introduced the features and amenities that are part of the NYCDOT/NYCT's BRT toolbox. These included: Offset and Curbside Bus Lanes, Off-Board Fare Collection, Enhanced Stations, Real-time Arrival Information, BRT Vehicles, Subway-like Station Spacing, and Traffic Signal Priority for Buses.

decisions.rationale: Blocks p005_c0008 and p005_c0010 discuss off-board fare collection as a BRT feature with participant comments. p005_c0008 only lists the feature in the toolbox. p005_c0010 contains participant comments asking about its success. Neither block explicitly states this treatment is attached to any specific corridor or project.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Facilitators introduced the features and amenities that are part of the NYCDOT/NYCT's BRT toolbox. These included: Offset and Curbside Bus Lanes, Off-Board Fare Collection, Enhanced Stations, Real-time Arrival Information, BRT Vehicles, Subway-like Station Spacing, and Traffic Signal Priority for Buses.
• Off-board fare payment would be particularly helpful. • Off-Board fare collection is good. • How successful is off-board fare collection in New York City?

decisions.rationale: Blocks p005_c0008, p005_c0010, and p006_c0006 discuss bus lanes as BRT features with participant comments. p005_c0008 only lists them in the toolbox. p005_c0010 includes participant skepticism and questions about bus lane locations. p006_c0006 includes a participant comment that Junction Boulevard is good for bus lanes. None of these blocks explicitly state bus lanes are being attached to any specific corridor or project.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Facilitators introduced the features and amenities that are part of the NYCDOT/NYCT's BRT toolbox. These included: Offset and Curbside Bus Lanes...
• Bus lanes do not work; local streets are too congested. • Where would bus lanes be? • Need to tie treatments to areas.
• Junction Boulevard is good for bus lanes.

decisions.rationale: Blocks p005_c0008 and p005_c0010 discuss traffic signal priority as a BRT feature. p005_c0008 only lists it in the toolbox. p005_c0010 includes a participant question about how it works without a bus lane. Neither block explicitly states this treatment is attached to any specific corridor or project.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Facilitators introduced the features and amenities that are part of the NYCDOT/NYCT's BRT toolbox. These included: ... Traffic Signal Priority for Buses.
• How does traffic signal priority work without a bus lane?

decisions.rationale: Blocks p005_c0008 and p006_c0009 discuss enhanced stations as a BRT feature. p005_c0008 only lists it in the toolbox. p006_c0009 reports survey results showing participants preferred enhanced stations. Neither block explicitly states this treatment is attached to any specific corridor or project.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Facilitators introduced the features and amenities that are part of the NYCDOT/NYCT's BRT toolbox. These included: Offset and Curbside Bus Lanes, Off-Board Fare Collection, Enhanced Stations, Real-time Arrival Information, BRT Vehicles, Subway-like Station Spacing, and Traffic Signal Priority for Buses.
In looking at the features and amenities that a BRT system could offer, the participants preferred Real Time Bus Information, Enhanced Stations, Off-set Bus Lanes and Off-Board Fair Collection.

decisions.rationale: Blocks p005_c0008 and p006_c0009 discuss real-time arrival information as a BRT feature. p005_c0008 only lists it in the toolbox. p006_c0009 reports survey results showing participants preferred real-time bus information. Neither block explicitly states this treatment is attached to any specific corridor or project.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Facilitators introduced the features and amenities that are part of the NYCDOT/NYCT's BRT toolbox. These included: ... Real-time Arrival Information ...
In looking at the features and amenities that a BRT system could offer, the participants preferred Real Time Bus Information, Enhanced Stations, Off-set Bus Lanes and Off-Board Fair Collection.
